mnemonics: JVM Cleans Garbage Automatically, Remember! (JVM: Java Virtual Machine, Cleans: Collection, Garbage: Garbage, Automatically: Automatic process, Remember: Mnemonic for recall)
keyPoints: Garbage collection is an automatic memory management process in Java that reclaims memory from objects no longer in use
An object becomes eligible for garbage collection when there are no more references to it
The finalize() method is called by the garbage collector before object destruction, but its execution is not guaranteed
commonMistakes: Assuming finalize() will always execute promptly or predictably
Manually trying to force garbage collection with System.gc() expecting immediate results
Creating memory leaks by holding unnecessary object references
